subject: general-knowledge
description: Security Alliances MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Today, how many countries are members of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO)?
mcqs.explanation: NATO currently has 32 member countries — Finland (2023) and Sweden (2024) are the newest.

mcqs.q: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) was established in:
mcqs.explanation: NATO was established on 4 April 1949 in Washington D.C.

mcqs.q: Which American president was also a Supreme Commander of NATO forces in Europe before his US presidency?
mcqs.explanation: Dwight D. Eisenhower served as the first Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR) before becoming US President in 1953.

mcqs.q: What is the objective behind the formation of NATO?
options: To develop individual and collective capacity to resist armed attack on any of the members
To consult and resist if any member is threatened politically
To consult and develop capacity to resist armed as well as political threat
mcqs.explanation: NATO's objective is to consult and develop capacity to resist both armed and political threats — the principle of collective defence (Article 5).

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a military alliance?
mcqs.explanation: NATO is the only military alliance among these — ASEAN, NAFTA and EEC are economic/regional groupings.

mcqs.q: Where are the NATO headquarters?
mcqs.explanation: NATO is headquartered in Brussels, Belgium.

mcqs.q: What two security organizations dominated the Cold War era?
options: Anti-Comintern Axis and League of Nations
mcqs.explanation: The Cold War was dominated by NATO (Western bloc) and the Warsaw Pact (Soviet/Eastern bloc).

mcqs.q: NATO was founded with the aim of increasing the West's ability to resist the threat of:
mcqs.explanation: NATO was founded in 1949 to counter the Soviet/Communist Bloc.

mcqs.q: Which of the following countries is NOT a member of NATO?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is not a NATO member — it was the successor to the USSR which led the Warsaw Pact.

mcqs.q: The Warsaw Pact was founded on which date?
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was signed on 14 May 1955 by the Soviet Union and 7 Eastern European allies.

mcqs.q: Where was the headquarters of the Warsaw Pact?
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was headquartered in Moscow.

mcqs.q: The Warsaw Pact was disestablished in:
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was dissolved on 1 July 1991 — following the collapse of communist regimes in Eastern Europe.

mcqs.q: Who is the current Secretary-General of NATO?
mcqs.explanation: Mark Rutte, former PM of the Netherlands, became NATO Secretary-General on 1 October 2024, succeeding Jens Stoltenberg.

mcqs.q: What do the initials CENTO stand for?
mcqs.explanation: CENTO = Central Treaty Organization, originally known as the Baghdad Pact.

mcqs.q: The Central Treaty Organization (CENTO) was established in which year?
mcqs.explanation: CENTO (originally the Baghdad Pact) was established in 1955.

mcqs.q: Which set of countries were the founders of CENTO?
options: USA, Turkey, Iran, Pakistan and Iraq
UK, Turkey, Iran, Pakistan and Syria
USA, France, Iran, Turkey and Pakistan
UK, Turkey, Iran, Pakistan and Iraq
mcqs.explanation: CENTO's founding members were the UK, Turkey, Iran, Pakistan and Iraq.

mcqs.q: The USA was NOT among the founders of CENTO but joined as an associate in:
mcqs.explanation: The USA joined CENTO as an associate member in 1958 — but was never a full member.

mcqs.q: When did Turkey, Iran and Pakistan withdraw from CENTO?
mcqs.explanation: Turkey, Iran and Pakistan withdrew from CENTO in 1979 — leading to its dissolution.

mcqs.q: Which country was very active in establishing CENTO but was not a full member?
mcqs.explanation: The USA was instrumental in establishing CENTO but never became a full member (only an associate, 1958).

mcqs.q: What do the initials SEATO stand for?
mcqs.explanation: SEATO = South-East Asia Treaty Organization — founded by the Manila Pact 1954/55.

mcqs.q: SEATO was formally founded in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was formally founded on 19 February 1955 in Bangkok (Manila Pact signed 8 Sep 1954).

mcqs.q: SEATO was founded by which pact?
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was created by the Manila Pact (Southeast Asia Collective Defense Treaty), signed in the Philippines on 8 September 1954.

mcqs.q: SEATO was founded by Australia, France, NZ, Pakistan, Philippines, Thailand, Britain and:
mcqs.explanation: The 8 founding SEATO members included the United States.

mcqs.q: SEATO's headquarters were in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was headquartered in Bangkok, Thailand.

mcqs.q: SEATO was dissolved in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO was dissolved in 1977 after losing relevance post-Vietnam War.

mcqs.q: What was the main purpose of SEATO?
options: Providing collective defence and enhancing economic co-operation in South-East Asia
Making SE Asia a free trade zone
Providing economic assistance to fight terrorism
mcqs.explanation: SEATO's primary purpose was collective defence (containment of communism) plus economic cooperation in SE Asia.

mcqs.q: When did Pakistan withdraw from SEATO?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan withdrew from SEATO in 1973 after the alliance failed to support it in the 1971 war with India.

mcqs.q: China founded the international alliance 'Shanghai Five' in which month/year?
mcqs.explanation: The Shanghai Five was founded in April 1996 in Shanghai, China.

mcqs.q: The original members of the Shanghai Five were Russia, China, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan and:
mcqs.explanation: Shanghai Five = Russia + China + Kazakhstan + Kyrgyzstan + Tajikistan.

mcqs.q: The Shanghai Five especially focused on battling terrorist threats from:
mcqs.explanation: The Shanghai Five focused on counter-terrorism, including threats from Afghanistan and Central Asian militancy.

mcqs.q: In June 2001, which Central Asian state joined the Shanghai Five, after which the group was renamed the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO)?
mcqs.explanation: Uzbekistan joined the Shanghai Five in June 2001, after which the group was renamed the SCO.

mcqs.q: What is the total number of member states in the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO)?
mcqs.explanation: The SCO has 8 main member states — India and Pakistan joined on 10 July 2015.

mcqs.q: What are the major objectives of the SCO?
mcqs.explanation: SCO's objectives include resolving border disputes, fostering economic ties, counter-terrorism (RATS) and promoting a multipolar world.
